objective: introduce a minimum review layer that validates generated spec artifacts before session execution
confirmed_facts: generated specs can drift in scope and acceptance quality
human review is still required but can be reduced by structured AI review
the current OS already has strong execution and validation layers
interpretation: a review layer can reduce human workload before implementation starts
structured rejection is more reusable than freeform critique
runtime integration should be deferred to avoid scope expansion
risks: the reviewer may still miss domain-specific scope problems
rejection categories may need expansion after real usage
without runtime integration the process remains partially manual
open_issues: builder and reviewer are not yet connected in a single operational flow
no automated validator exists for reviewer outputs
human final approval is still required
next_actions: connect spec builder and spec reviewer flow in session-39
define pass JSON template for approved generated specs
evaluate optional runtime integration after manual flow stabilizes

final_judgement.reason: minimum review-layer design artifacts are established as docs-only outputs covering prompt, output contract, and structured rejection format
